summary: Labeled several OSResetSW and OS time-related sbss symbols, replacing anonymous lbl_* references with meaningful names and folding adjacent 32-bit labels into 64-bit storage where the code clearly accessed high/low halves via symbol+4. The PR renamed reset-switch state globals such as ResetCallback, Down, HoldUp, HoldDown, and consolidated __OSStartTime to cover its second word. Review noted these globals should ultimately become static when OSResetSW is converted to C.
what_changed: Renamed sbss label lbl_804D73A8 to ResetCallback in OSResetSW data and all inline assembly references.
Renamed sbss label lbl_804D73AC to Down in OSResetSW data and all inline assembly references.
Collapsed HoldUp plus lbl_804D73BC into a single 8-byte HoldUp allocation, with former lbl_804D73BC accesses rewritten as HoldUp+4.
Collapsed HoldDown plus lbl_804D73C4 into a single 8-byte HoldDown allocation, with former lbl_804D73C4 accesses rewritten as HoldDown+4.
Collapsed __OSStartTime plus lbl_804D7354 into a single 8-byte __OSStartTime allocation, with former lbl_804D7354 accesses rewritten as __OSStartTime+4.
Removed extern declarations for the eliminated lbl_* symbols in src/dolphin/os/OS.c and src/dolphin/os/OSResetSW.c.
Kept the functions themselves as inline assembly while improving symbol names inside OSInit, __OSResetSWInterruptHandler, and OSGetResetButtonState.
smart_moves: Used symbol+4 references instead of keeping artificial labels for the second word of 64-bit values, making the data layout clearer without changing addresses.
Recognized paired 32-bit sbss words as single 8-byte logical values for HoldUp, HoldDown, and __OSStartTime.
Renamed only labels supported by usage patterns in the existing assembly, keeping the patch narrow and low risk.
Prepared OSResetSW globals for a later C conversion by assigning semantic names before attempting a larger decompilation rewrite.
decomp_lessons: When two adjacent lbl_* sbss symbols are always used as low/high words of a time-like value, prefer a single named allocation plus explicit +4 references.
Labeling globals in assembly data can be a useful intermediate step before converting an asm function to C.
Removing dead externs for eliminated lbl_* symbols keeps C files aligned with the actual data model and prevents stale names from spreading.
For small-data r13 references, symbol+offset can document structure or 64-bit scalar layout while preserving exact instruction encodings.
naming_conventions: Use OSResetSW_LastState for the exported/reset-switch last-state global already present in the data file.
Use ResetCallback for the reset-switch callback pointer formerly named lbl_804D73A8.
Use Down for the reset-switch down-state flag formerly named lbl_804D73AC.
Use HoldUp and HoldDown as 8-byte time/state storage symbols, referring to their second word as HoldUp+4 and HoldDown+4.
Use __OSStartTime as an 8-byte OS start time symbol, referring to its second word as __OSStartTime+4 instead of a separate lbl_*.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Preserve inline assembly while replacing operands such as lbl_804D73C4(r13) with HoldDown+4(r13).
Preserve instruction comments and addresses while changing only symbolic operands.
Use .skip 0x8 to combine adjacent .skip 0x4 allocations when they represent a single logical object.
Avoid introducing C definitions prematurely; keep temporary assembly globals until a later conversion can make them static.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: Asked whether the newly named OSResetSW data symbols should be static.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.comment: Confirmed the symbols would all become static when converted to C in a subsequent PR.

follow_up_queries: Find the subsequent PR that converts OSResetSW globals to static C definitions.
Search for remaining references to lbl_804D73A8, lbl_804D73AC, lbl_804D73BC, lbl_804D73C4, or lbl_804D7354.
Check whether HoldUp, HoldDown, and __OSStartTime later become typed 64-bit time values.
Look for other Dolphin OS sbss pairs that should be collapsed into named 8-byte symbols with +4 references.
